title: Cache and downloads
text: call datasus_download() use_cache = TRUE (default), files stored structured subdirectory tree cache folder. subsequent calls files, cached versions reused without network access.
code: library(datasusr)  downloads <- datasus_fetch(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = c("RD", "SP"),   year      = 2024,   month     = 1,   uf        = c("PE", "PB") )

title: Cache and downloads
text: cache location resolved following order: cache_dir function argument DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable datasusr.cache_dir R option default user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("datasusr", "cache")) set globally, add line .Renviron: R:
code: DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR=/path/to/my/cache options(datasusr.cache_dir = "/path/to/my/cache")

title: Cache and downloads
code: # Quick summary datasus_cache_info(verbose = TRUE)  # Detailed listing of all cached files datasus_cache_list()

title: Cache and downloads
text: Pass refresh = TRUE datasus_download() (datasus_fetch()) re-download files even exist cache:
code: datasus_download(files, refresh = TRUE)

title: Cache and downloads
text: time cache can grow large. Two functions help manage size: pruning size, least-recently-accessed files removed first.
code: # Remove files older than 90 days datasus_cache_prune(older_than_days = 90)  # Keep the total cache under 5 GB datasus_cache_prune(max_size_bytes = 5 * 1024^3)  # Remove everything datasus_cache_clear()

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: packages ultimately use C code based Mark Adler’s blast decompressor PKWare DCL format used DATASUS. key difference decompressed data handled: microdatasus delegates reading read.dbc package, decompresses .dbc temporary .dbf file disk, calls foreign::read.dbf() parse data.frame. two-step process involves disk /O inherits type limitations foreign::read.dbf() (numeric fields become double). datasusr performs decompression DBF parsing entirely memory single C call. compressed payload decompressed directly memory buffer parsed field--field pre-allocated R vectors. avoids temporary files enables features like column selection, type inference, date parsing C level. also handles wider range DBC variants, including files non-standard header terminators found CNES files.

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: packages provide one-step function download read files: packages differ design: microdatasus uses single function (fetch_datasus()) parameters upfront. system name uses hyphenated format ("SIH-RD", "SIM-", "CNES-ST"). Path construction handled internally. simple effective common use cases. datasusr offers layered API can used level granularity: use datasus_fetch() one call, just like microdatasus.
code: # --- datasusr ----------------------------------------------------------------- library(datasusr) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RD",   year = 2024, month = 1:3, uf = "PE",   select = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "munic_res", "val_tot") )  # --- microdatasus ------------------------------------------------------------- library(microdatasus) df <- fetch_datasus(   year_start = 2024, year_end = 2024,   month_start = 1, month_end = 3,   uf = "PE", information_system = "SIH-RD" ) df <- process_sih(df) # Browse what's available datasus_sources() datasus_file_types(source = "SINAN")  # Check which files exist on the FTP files <- datasus_list_files(   source = "SINAN", file_type = "DENG",   year = 2020:2024 )  # Download with parallel connections and progress bars downloads <- datasus_download(files)  # Read with type control df <- read_datasus_dbc(   downloads$local_file[[1]],   select    = c("dt_notific", "id_municip", "classi_fin"),   col_types = c(dt_notific = "date"),   parse_dates = TRUE )

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: microdatasus downloads files temporary directory (tempdir()). Files preserved R sessions, re-running query triggers new download DATASUS FTP. datasusr maintains persistent cache directory survives sessions. Repeated calls parameters reuse cached files without network access. cache location can configured via DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable, datasusr.cache_dir R option, cache_dir argument. Management functions let inspect, prune, clear cache:
code: datasus_cache_info()                         # summary datasus_cache_prune(older_than_days = 90)    # remove old files datasus_cache_prune(max_size_bytes = 5e9)    # cap total size datasus_cache_clear()                        # remove everything

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: microdatasus provides significant added value. process_*() functions decode categorical fields replacing numeric codes human-readable labels. example, process_sim() converts ICD-10 codes descriptions, municipality codes names, numeric sex/race indicators labelled factors. done SIM, SINASC, SIH, SIA, CNES, several SINAN diseases. datasusr perform variable labelling. returns raw values DBC files, leaving semantic interpretation analyst. need labelled variables, can use territorial tables lookup references:
code: library(dplyr)  municipios <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip")  df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DO",   year = 2022, uf = "PE" ) |>   left_join(     select(municipios, co_municip, ds_nome),     by = c("codmunocor" = "co_municip")   )

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: writing, microdatasus archived CRAN depends read.dbc, also archived. remain installable GitHub. datasusr available GitHub dependency either package — bundles C code DBC decompression.

title: Comparison with microdatasus
text: Choose datasusr need: Fast reads large DBC files column selection type control Persistent caching avoid re-downloading Exploratory browsing DATASUS catalog FTP Parallel downloads progress bars Territorial reference tables (municipalities, health regions) tidyverse-native workflow snake_case column names Choose microdatasus need: Automatic variable labelling categorical recoding Analysis-ready data frames minimal code well-documented, published package (Cad. Saude Publica, 2019) two packages can also complement : use datasusr fast, cached /O microdatasus::process_*() post-processing needed.

title: Available data sources
text: access column indicates function use source: fetch — use datasus_fetch(), step--step workflow datasus_list_files(), datasus_download(), read_datasus_dbc(). sources contain .dbc files DATASUS FTP. territory — use datasus_get_territory(). CSV reference tables (municipalities, health regions, etc.). ftp_only — use datasus_ftp_ls() browse. software downloads (TabWin, TabNet), data files.
code: library(datasusr)  datasus_sources()

title: Available data sources
text: Hospital Information System (SIHSUS) publishes monthly files state.
code: # Reduced Hospital Admission Records df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RD",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE",   select = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "munic_res", "val_tot") )  # Rejected admissions df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RJ",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Professional services df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "SP",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: Outpatient Information System (SIASUS) also publishes monthly files state.
code: # Outpatient production df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIASUS", file_type = "PA",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Medication authorisations (APAC) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIASUS", file_type = "AM",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: Mortality Information System (SIM) publishes yearly files. Death records () scoped state; specialised subsets (DOFET, DOEXT, DOINF, DOMAT) cover Brazil.
code: # Death records by state (4-digit year in file name) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DO",   year = 2022, uf = "PE" )  # Foetal deaths df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DOFET",   year = 2022 )  # Deaths from external causes df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DOEXT",   year = 2022 )  # Infant deaths df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DOINF",   year = 2022 )  # Maternal deaths df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SIM", file_type = "DOMAT",   year = 2022 )

title: Available data sources
text: Live Birth Information System publishes yearly files state.
code: df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SINASC", file_type = "DN",   year = 2022, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: National Registry Health Facilities publishes monthly files state across many subtypes. See datasus_file_types(source = "CNES") full list CNES subtypes (LT, ST, DC, EQ, SR, HB, PF, EP, RC, , EE, EF, GM).
code: # Facilities df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CNES", file_type = "ST",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Hospital beds df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CNES", file_type = "LT",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Professionals df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CNES", file_type = "PF",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Equipment df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CNES", file_type = "EQ",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # Specialised services df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CNES", file_type = "SR",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: CIHA replaced CIH 2011. publish monthly files state.
code: # CIHA (2011 onwards) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CIHA", file_type = "CIHA",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" )  # CIH (historical, 2008-2011) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "CIH", file_type = "CR",   year = 2010, month = 1, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: SINAN publishes yearly files national scope (UF filter needed). Preliminary SINAN data available SINAN_P source.
code: # Dengue df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SINAN", file_type = "DENG",   year = 2023 )  # Chikungunya df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SINAN", file_type = "CHIK",   year = 2023 )  # Zika df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SINAN", file_type = "ZIKA",   year = 2023 )  # Malaria df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SINAN", file_type = "MALA",   year = 2023 )

title: Available data sources
code: # e-SUS Notifica (chronic Chagas disease) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "ESUSNOTIFICA", file_type = "DCCR",   year = 2023 )  # Suspected congenital Zika syndrome (RESP) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "RESP", file_type = "RESP",   year = 2022, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
code: df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "PO", file_type = "PO",   year = 2022 )

title: Available data sources
code: df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "PCE", file_type = "PCE",   year = 2022, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: SISCOLO SISMAMA replaced SISCAN longer available DATASUS FTP. SISPRENATAL data may still available historical periods.
code: # Prenatal monitoring (historical) df <- datasus_fetch(   source = "SISPRENATAL", file_type = "PN",   year = 2014, month = 1, uf = "PE" )

title: Available data sources
text: Territorial data (municipality names, health regions, geographic divisions) published CSV files organised year. Use datasus_get_territory():
code: # Municipality table (defaults to current year) municipalities <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip") municipalities  # Specific year municipalities_2023 <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip", year = 2023)  # Browse available years and tables datasus_ftp_ls("ftp://ftp.datasus.gov.br/territorio/tabelas/")

title: Available data sources
text: information system documentation files DATASUS FTP. Use datasus_docs_url() find :
code: # All known documentation paths datasus_docs_url()  # List documentation files for a specific system datasus_ftp_ls(datasus_docs_url("CNES")$docs_url[[1]])

title: Available data sources
text: following code tests path resolution every source file type catalog:
code: library(dplyr)  sources_dbc <- datasus_sources() |>   filter(access == "fetch")  results <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(sources_dbc)), \(i) {   src <- sources_dbc$source[[i]]   fts <- datasus_file_types(source = src)    purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(fts)), \(j) {     ft <- fts$file_type[[j]]     ok <- tryCatch({       datasus_build_path(source = src, file_type = ft, year = 2023, month = 1)       TRUE     }, error = function(e) FALSE)     tibble::tibble(source = src, file_type = ft, has_path = ok)   }) |> purrr::list_rbind() }) |> purrr::list_rbind()  results |> print(n = Inf)

title: Available data sources
text: examples download files local cache. remove cached files testing:
code: datasus_cache_info() datasus_cache_clear()

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: know source, file type, period, state need, datasus_fetch() handles listing, downloading, reading single call: result tibble ready analysis dplyr, ggplot2, tidyverse tool. Files cached default, running call skips download entirely.
code: library(datasusr)  df <- datasus_fetch(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1,   uf        = "PE" )  df

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: already .dbc file disk, use read_datasus_dbc() directly:
code: x <- read_datasus_dbc("RDPE2401.dbc") x

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: DATASUS files often dozens columns. Use select keep need — faster uses less memory:
code: x <- read_datasus_dbc(   "RDPE2401.dbc",   select = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "munic_res", "val_tot") )

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: default, datasusr inspects numeric field decide integer double. can override col_types parse date fields parse_dates:
code: x <- read_datasus_dbc(   "SPPE2401.dbc",   select     = c("sp_gestor", "sp_naih", "sp_dtinter", "sp_valato"),   col_types  = c(     sp_gestor  = "character",     sp_naih    = "character",     sp_dtinter = "date",     sp_valato  = "double"   ),   parse_dates = TRUE,   guess_types = FALSE )

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: downloading, can browse internal catalog discover sources file types available:
code: datasus_sources() datasus_file_types(source = "SIHSUS") datasus_file_types(source = "CNES")

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: control, can use individual functions instead datasus_fetch(): skip FTP validation (useful server slow), set check_exists = FALSE datasus_list_files().
code: # 1. Build the FTP paths datasus_build_path(source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RD", year = 2024, month = 1)  # 2. List files (validated against FTP) files <- datasus_list_files(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1:3,   uf        = c("PE", "PB") )  # 3. Download with cache downloads <- datasus_download(files, use_cache = TRUE)  # 4. Read x <- read_datasus_dbc(downloads$local_file[[1]])

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: DATASUS publishes territorial reference tables (municipalities, health regions, etc.) CSV files. Use datasus_get_territory() download read :
code: # Download municipalities table municipios <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip") municipios  # Other available tables datasus_ftp_ls("ftp://ftp.datasus.gov.br/territorio/tabelas/")

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: information system documentation files DATASUS FTP. Use datasus_docs_url() find :
code: # See all documentation paths datasus_docs_url()  # List documentation files for a specific system datasus_docs_url("CNES") datasus_ftp_ls(datasus_docs_url("CNES")$docs_url[[1]])

title: Getting started with datasusr
text: See vignettes detail: Cache downloads — caching works manage Performance notes — tips reading large files quickly Comparison read.dbc microdatasus — datasusr relates R packages DATASUS data

title: Performance notes
text: single effective way speed reads select columns need. C parser skips unneeded fields byte level, fewer columns means less allocation less parsing:
code: library(datasusr)  # Slow: reads all ~100+ columns x <- read_datasus_dbc("RDPE2401.dbc")  # Fast: reads only 4 columns x <- read_datasus_dbc(   "RDPE2401.dbc",   select = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "munic_res", "val_tot") )

title: Performance notes
text: guess_types = TRUE (default), reader scans every value numeric field decide whether fits integer requires double. Disabling skips scan relies DBF field metadata alone: safe analyses can save noticeable time files millions rows.
code: x <- read_datasus_dbc("RDPE2401.dbc", guess_types = FALSE)

title: Performance notes
text: know exactly types need, col_types lets specify upfront skip inference entirely:
code: x <- read_datasus_dbc(   "RDPE2401.dbc",   select      = c("uf_zi", "dt_inter", "val_tot"),   col_types   = c(uf_zi = "character", dt_inter = "date", val_tot = "double"),   parse_dates = TRUE,   guess_types = FALSE )

title: Performance notes
text: Date parsing (parse_dates = TRUE) adds small overhead per date field. enable actually need Date objects; otherwise raw YYYYMMDD character strings often sufficient filtering grouping.

title: Performance notes
code: library(bench)  file <- "RDPE2401.dbc"  bench::mark(   default    = read_datasus_dbc(file),   no_guess   = read_datasus_dbc(file, guess_types = FALSE),   selected   = read_datasus_dbc(file, select = c("uf_zi", "val_tot")),   typed      = read_datasus_dbc(     file,     select      = c("uf_zi", "dt_inter", "val_tot"),     col_types   = c(uf_zi = "character", dt_inter = "date", val_tot = "double"),     parse_dates = TRUE,     guess_types = FALSE   ),   check      = FALSE,   iterations = 5 )

title: Performance notes
text: datasusr allocates R vectors directly C without materialising intermediate files disk. large datasets (tens millions rows), consider processing files batches rather binding everything single data frame.

what: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
text: datasusr provides fast, -memory reading DATASUS .dbc files complete workflow discovering, downloading, caching, reading Brazilian public health data DATASUS FTP.

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/datasusr")

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
code: library(datasusr)  # One-step: list, download, and read SIH data for Pernambuco df <- datasus_fetch(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1,   uf        = "PE" )  df

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
text: control, use individual functions:
code: library(datasusr)  # 1. Explore the catalog datasus_sources() datasus_file_types(source = "SIHSUS")  # 2. List available files on the FTP files <- datasus_list_files(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1:3,   uf        = c("PE", "PB") )  # 3. Download (with automatic caching) downloads <- datasus_download(files, use_cache = TRUE)  # 4. Read a DBC file into a tibble x <- read_datasus_dbc(downloads$local_file[[1]])  # 5. Read with column selection and type control x <- read_datasus_dbc(   downloads$local_file[[1]],   select     = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "dt_inter", "val_tot"),   col_types  = c(dt_inter = "date", val_tot = "double"),   parse_dates = TRUE )

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
text: Downloads cached default repeated runs hit DATASUS FTP: can configure cache directory via DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable, datasusr.cache_dir R option, cache_dir argument.
code: datasus_cache_info() datasus_cache_list()  # Prune old files datasus_cache_prune(older_than_days = 90)  # Or clear everything datasus_cache_clear()

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
text: functions emit cli progress messages default. Suppress verbose = FALSE.

title: datasusr - Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from DATASUS
text: MIT

what: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
title: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
text: Builds candidate FTP directories given source file type. SIH/SIA, function selects historical vs. current trees year/month. SIM SINASC, preliminary trees included requested.

title: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
code: datasus_build_path(   source,   file_type,   year = NULL,   month = NULL,   include_prelim = TRUE )

title: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
text: source Source code (e.g. "SIHSUS"). file_type File type code (e.g. "RD"). year Integer vector years. month Integer vector months. include_prelim Logical. Include preliminary trees available (default TRUE).

title: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
text: tibble columns source, file_type, period, path.

title: Build DATASUS FTP paths from filters — datasus_build_path
code: datasus_build_path(source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RD", year = 2024, month = 1) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   source file_type period  path                                                  #>   <chr>  <chr>     <chr>   <chr>                                                 #> 1 SIHSUS NA        current ftp://ftp.datasus.gov.br/dissemin/publicos/SIHSUS/20…

text: Removes files cache directory. default cached files removed; pass character vector files remove specific paths.

code: datasus_cache_clear(cache_dir = NULL, files = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Clear cached DATASUS files — datasus_cache_clear
text: cache_dir Optional cache directory. files Optional character vector file paths remove. NULL, cached files removed. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Clear cached DATASUS files — datasus_cache_clear
text: tibble columns path removed.

title: Prune the datasusr cache — datasus_cache_prune
text: Selectively removes cached files based age /total size. Older least-recently-accessed files removed first.

title: Prune the datasusr cache — datasus_cache_prune
code: datasus_cache_prune(   cache_dir = NULL,   max_size_bytes = NULL,   older_than_days = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Prune the datasusr cache — datasus_cache_prune
text: cache_dir Optional cache directory. max_size_bytes Maximum total cache size bytes. exceeded, least-recently-accessed files removed cache fits. older_than_days Age threshold days. Files modification time older removed. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Prune the datasusr cache — datasus_cache_prune
text: tibble columns path, removed, reason.

title: Get FTP URLs for DATASUS documentation — datasus_docs_url

title: Get FTP URLs for DATASUS documentation — datasus_docs_url

title: Download DATASUS files — datasus_download
text: Downloads one many DATASUS files. use_cache = TRUE, files already exist cache directory reused instead re-downloaded.

title: Download DATASUS files — datasus_download
code: datasus_download(   files = NULL,   ...,   dest_dir = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   timeout = 240,   use_cache = TRUE,   cache_dir = NULL,   refresh = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download DATASUS files — datasus_download
text: files tibble returned datasus_list_files(). NULL, additional filters forwarded datasus_list_files(). ... Filters passed datasus_list_files() files NULL. dest_dir Optional destination directory. NULL use_cache = TRUE, package cache directory used. overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing files (default FALSE). timeout Timeout seconds download (default 240). use_cache Logical. Store reuse downloads cache directory (default TRUE). cache_dir Optional cache directory. refresh Logical. Force re-download even cached file exists (default FALSE). verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Download DATASUS files — datasus_download
text: tibble local_file column containing paths downloaded files, plus downloaded flag.

title: Download DATASUS files — datasus_download
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ files <- datasus_list_files(   source = "SIHSUS", file_type = "RD",   year = 2024, month = 1, uf = "PE" ) downloads <- datasus_download(files) } # }

what: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
title: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
text: convenience wrapper lists, downloads, reads DATASUS files single call. Particularly useful interactive / exploratory work.

title: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
code: datasus_fetch(   source,   file_type,   year = NULL,   month = NULL,   uf = NULL,   ...,   bind = TRUE,   include_prelim = TRUE,   timeout = 240,   use_cache = TRUE,   cache_dir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
text: source Character vector source codes. file_type Character vector file type codes. year Integer vector years. month Integer vector months (required monthly sources). uf Character vector UF codes (required UF-scoped sources). ... Additional arguments forwarded read_datasus_dbc() (e.g. select, col_types, parse_dates). bind Logical. TRUE (default), files row-bound single tibble. FALSE, list tibbles returned. include_prelim Logical. Include preliminary data trees (default TRUE). timeout Timeout seconds FTP download operations (default 240). use_cache Logical. Reuse cached downloads (default TRUE). cache_dir Optional cache directory. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
text: tibble (bind = TRUE) named list tibbles.

title: Fetch DATASUS data in one step — datasus_fetch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fetch SIH data for Pernambuco, Jan 2024 df <- datasus_fetch(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1,   uf        = "PE" )  # Fetch with column selection (snake_case names work) df <- datasus_fetch(   source    = "SIHSUS",   file_type = "RD",   year      = 2024,   month     = 1:3,   uf        = "PE",   select    = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "munic_res", "val_tot") ) } # }

title: Download DATASUS territorial tables — datasus_get_territory
text: Downloads reads tables DATASUS territorial data section. DATASUS FTP publishes ZIP archive per year containing reference tables municipalities, health regions, geographic divisions used SUS, CSV, DBF, TXT formats.

title: Download DATASUS territorial tables — datasus_get_territory
code: datasus_get_territory(   table = "tb_municip",   year = NULL,   format = "csv",   cache_dir = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download DATASUS territorial tables — datasus_get_territory
text: table Name table read. Common values: "tb_municip" (municipalities), "tb_uf" (states), "tb_regsaud" (health regions), "tb_macsaud" (macro health regions), "tb_regiao" (geographic regions), "tb_micibge" (IBGE micro-regions), "tb_regmetr" (metropolitan regions), "tb_dsei" (indigenous health districts). Relationship tables start "rl_" (e.g. "rl_municip_regsaud"). year Year territorial table. Defaults current year. Use datasus_ftp_ls("ftp://ftp.datasus.gov.br/territorio/tabelas/") see available years. format File format extract ZIP: "csv" (default) "dbf". cache_dir Optional cache directory. verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Download DATASUS territorial tables — datasus_get_territory
text: tibble column names snake_case.

title: Download DATASUS territorial tables — datasus_get_territory
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Municipalities municipios <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip")  # Health regions regioes <- datasus_get_territory("tb_regsaud")  # States ufs <- datasus_get_territory("tb_uf")  # Specific year municipios_2023 <- datasus_get_territory("tb_municip", year = 2023)  # List available years datasus_ftp_ls("ftp://ftp.datasus.gov.br/territorio/tabelas/") } # }

title: List available DATASUS files — datasus_list_files

title: List available DATASUS files — datasus_list_files
text: source Character vector source codes. file_type Character vector file type codes. year Integer vector years. month Integer vector months (required monthly sources). uf Character vector UF codes (required UF-scoped sources). include_prelim Logical. Include preliminary data trees (default TRUE). check_exists Logical. Query FTP keep files exist (default TRUE). Setting FALSE skips FTP access returns candidate files. timeout Timeout seconds FTP requests (default 120). ftp_use_epsv Logical. Passed curl (default FALSE). verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

text: tibble one row per file, including FTP URL metadata.

what: datasusr: Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from 'DATASUS' — datasusr-package
title: datasusr: Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from 'DATASUS' — datasusr-package
text: Provides fast, -memory reading DATASUS DBC files using native 'C' code, along catalog public health data sources, 'FTP' file discovery, caching downloads, high-level 'datasus_fetch()' function lists, downloads, reads files single call. Bundles 'blast' decompressor 'zlib' contrib/blast decode 'PKWare DCL'-compressed 'DBC' files parses 'DBF' records directly efficient import tibbles.

title: datasusr: Fast Access to Brazilian Public Health Data from 'DATASUS' — datasusr-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Marcos Wasilew marcos.wasilew@gmail.com Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Carlos Amorin carlos.agaf@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br contributors: Mark Adler (Author bundled blast.c blast.h zlib contrib/blast) [contributor, copyright holder]

title: Read DATASUS DBC files — read_datasus_dbc
text: Reads DATASUS .dbc file directly R using native C code PKWare DCL decompression DBF parsing. function always returns tibble designed work well tidyverse.

title: Read DATASUS DBC files — read_datasus_dbc
code: read_datasus_dbc(   file,   select = NULL,   n_max = Inf,   trim_ws = TRUE,   encoding = "latin1",   guess_types = TRUE,   col_types = NULL,   parse_dates = FALSE,   clean_names = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read DATASUS DBC files — read_datasus_dbc
text: file Path .dbc .dbf file. compressed (DBC) uncompressed (DBF) formats accepted. select Optional character vector column names keep. NULL (default), columns returned. Names matched case-insensitively, "UF_ZI" "uf_zi" work. n_max Maximum number rows read. Defaults Inf (rows). trim_ws Logical. Trim leading/trailing whitespace character fields (default TRUE). encoding Encoding DBF character fields. Typically "latin1" (default DATASUS files) "UTF-8". guess_types Logical. Inspect numeric fields distinguish integer-like columns double columns (default TRUE). Disable faster reads precise types needed. col_types Optional named character vector explicit column types. Supported values: "character", "integer", "double", "logical", "date". Names matched case-insensitively original DBF field names. parse_dates Logical. TRUE, DBF date fields (D type) returned Date. Character fields parsed dates explicitly listed col_types (default FALSE). clean_names Logical. TRUE (default), column names converted snake_case (lowercase underscores). original DATASUS names uppercase (e.g. UF_ZI becomes uf_zi). verbose Logical. Emit progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Read DATASUS DBC files — read_datasus_dbc
text: tibble.

title: Read DATASUS DBC files — read_datasus_dbc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic read (column names in snake_case by default) x <- read_datasus_dbc("RDPE2401.dbc") # columns: uf_zi, ano_cmpt, val_tot, ...  # Select works with either case x <- read_datasus_dbc(   "RDPE2401.dbc",   select = c("uf_zi", "ano_cmpt", "val_tot") )  # Keep original uppercase names x <- read_datasus_dbc("RDPE2401.dbc", clean_names = FALSE) # columns: UF_ZI, ANO_CMPT, VAL_TOT, ... } # }

title: datasusr 0.1.0
text: Package version bumped 0.1.0 reflect expanded scope beyond just reading DBC files.

title: datasusr 0.1.0
text: datasus_fetch(): high-level convenience function lists, downloads, reads DATASUS files single call. Supports column selection, type specification, row-binding multiple files. DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable: cache directory now respects DATASUSR_CACHE_DIR env var addition existing datasusr.cache_dir option. format_bytes() now exported general use.

title: datasusr 0.1.0
text: Fixed SIM path templates: DOEXT, DOINF, DOMAT previously pointed DOFET FTP directory. now correct path. Fixed column/argument name shadowing datasus_build_path(), datasus_file_types(), datasus_list_files(). Column names like source file_type longer clash function arguments thanks proper use .env$ pronouns. Download failures longer abort loop: individual FTP download errors caught reported without stopping remaining downloads. Empty file lists now return immediately datasus_download() instead proceeding zero iterations.

title: datasusr 0.1.0
text: Removed .data$ pronoun usage inside dplyr verbs. Columns now referenced bare (tidy evaluation) names, external variables use .env$ needed. follows current tidyverse best practice. Replaced deprecated purrr functions: pmap_dfr() replaced map2() |> list_rbind(). Replaced purrr::map_lgl() cache functions base vapply() fewer dependencies hot paths. Improved CLI messages throughout: user-facing messages now use cli::cli_abort() structured hints (bullets) instead bare stop(). Progress messages concise informative. Wrapped FTP connection errors user-friendly message suggesting connectivity check. rlang now explicit dependency (already indirect dep via dplyr), making %||% import explicit. Added rlang (>= 1.0.0) dplyr (>= 1.1.0) minimum version constraints. Vignettes expanded richer explanations new comparison article. pkgdown site structure updated comparison article. Compiled .o .files excluded source bundle via .Rbuildignore.

title: datasusr 0.0.7.3
text: Fixed dplyr data-masking bugs catalog file listing helpers. Removed .data$ usage inside mutate pipelines simplified tidyverse expressions. Improved verbose output read_datasus_dbc() row column counts print separate lines.

title: datasusr 0.0.7.1
text: Fixed datasus_file_types(source = ...) filtering consistency. Improved cli spacing summary formatting key functions. Filtered invalid source/file type combinations earlier datasus_list_files().

title: datasusr 0.0.7
text: Standardized user-facing progress status messages cli. Added progress bars cache-hit messages datasus_download(). Added optional verbose control FTP, listing, cache helpers. Improved cache summaries human-readable sizes.

title: datasusr 0.0.6
text: Added configurable cache layer DATASUS downloads. Added datasus_cache_dir(), datasus_cache_list(), datasus_cache_info(), datasus_cache_clear(), datasus_cache_prune(). Integrated cache-aware downloads datasus_download().

title: datasusr 0.0.5
text: Added DATASUS catalog helpers: datasus_sources(), datasus_modalities(), datasus_file_types(), datasus_ufs(). Added FTP helpers: datasus_ftp_ls(), datasus_build_path(), datasus_list_files(), datasus_download().

title: datasusr 0.0.4
text: Added guess_types, col_types, parse_dates read_datasus_dbc(). Improved DBC handling files DBF headers plus compressed record areas.
